Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The model has some intentional anomalies 1 A loop between Evaluate E and Approve P 2 An XOR between Notify Customer N and skip SilentTransition 3 A partial order where Close Claim C can happen right after Assign Adjuster A bypassing the loop and XOR or concurrently with them

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to do three things 1 Identify the anomalies in the POWL model 2 Generate hypotheses on why these anomalies might exist in the real world process or the model 3 Propose database queries against the provided schema claims adjusters and claim events to verify these hypotheses by looking for actual occurrences of these anomalies in the event logs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Loop between Evaluate E and Approve P The OperatorPOWL operator Operator LOOP children E P means E is executed then optionally P then E then P etc In a normal claim process you evaluate then approve or reject Looping back to evaluate after approval does not make logical sense unless it is a reevaluation after an approval is contested but typically it would be Evaluate Approve XOR Reject

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: XOR skipping Notify Customer N The OperatorPOWL operator Operator XOR children N skip means the customer might not be notified In insurance notifying the customer of the claim outcome is usually a mandatory compliance and customer service step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Partial Ordering Anomaly Premature Closure The StrictPartialOrder has edges R to A A to loop loop to xor but also A to C Because C is in the same partial order and only constrained to be after A and not strictly after xor C can execute immediately after A bypassing evaluation approval and notification It could also execute concurrently with the loop or xor

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Loop E and P Hypothesis 1 Business Rule Change A new policy requires secondary approval or reevaluation for claims above a certain threshold but it was modeled as a general loop Hypothesis 2 System Technical Error The workflow system automatically retriggers the evaluation step if the approval step fails to sync with the payment system creating an unintended loop in the event log Hypothesis 3 User Behavior Workaround Adjusters approve a claim realize they made a mistake and manually revert it to evaluate to fix the amount then approve again

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Skipping Notification N Hypothesis 1 Miscommunication Incomplete Design The notification is handled by an external system that does not log back to the main claim system so it looks like a skip in the main event log Hypothesis 2 Business Rule For very small claims or specific claim types notification is intentionally skipped Hypothesis 3 Technical Error The notification service frequently fails or times out and the system is configured to silently skip and close the claim rather than blocking

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Premature Closure A to C Hypothesis 1 Process Bypass Workaround Adjusters or managers have a fast track or cancel button to close invalid or duplicate claims immediately after assignment without going through full evaluation Hypothesis 2 Inadequate Constraints The process modeler tool did not enforce a strict sequence and the developer accidentally linked C to A instead of the final node Hypothesis 3 Automated Cancellation If a customer withdraws the claim right after assignment an automated script closes it immediately

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 1 Identify claims closed without proper evaluation or approval Premature Closure Look for claims that have Close Claim but lack Evaluate Claim or Approve Claim

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 2 Locate claims that were approved multiple times Loop Anomaly Count the number of Approve Claim events per claim To see the sequence of Evaluate and Approve

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 3 Check if customer notification steps are frequently skipped Compare total closed claims vs claims with Notify Customer To see if skips correlate with specific claim types or amounts

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 4 Check for Cancel or Withdraw in additional info for prematurely closed claims To verify the hypothesis that premature closures are actually cancellations

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Activities in DB likely map to R A E P N C or their full names Receive Claim Assign Adjuster etc I will use the full names for readability in SQL or the letters if specified

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refining the POWL anomaly identification Operator LOOP with E P means execute E then optionally execute P and loop back to E so Evaluate is always executed at least once Approve is executed zero or more times If it executes P it must execute E again This is highly anomalous because after approval you do not evaluate again Operator XOR with N skip means Notify is optional StrictPartialOrder with R to A A to loop loop to xor and A to C means C is only ordered after A so it can happen before loop concurrently with loop or after xor

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The queries look solid I will present them clearly with explanations of what each query achieves and how it maps to the hypotheses
